roadmap.goal: Slice 02 - blocked-time history capture + per-item duration
roadmap.overview: One TDD slice split into six steps. Step 02-01 lays the capture substrate (entity, migration, domain-event handlers, leave detection). Steps 02-02 through 02-05 enable the four existing [Ignore]-pending backend acceptance scenarios one at a time, each driving a new read-surface contract invariant (blockedSince present, null on unblock, null on first-observation, null when not rule-matched). Step 02-06 is the frontend Vitest step for the 'blocked <N>d' badge copy and the sync-cadence tooltip.

validation.notes: Created directly because des-roadmap is unavailable. Pending nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer review before execution.

out_of_scope: Cumulative duration across multiple past blocked spells (slice 02 surfaces the current spell only)
Lighthouse-Clients CLI/MCP wrapper changes for blockedSince (DDD-7 classifies it as additive, no version gate)
Playwright E2E for slice 02 (covered by backend acceptance tests + frontend Vitest)
RBAC changes (reuses existing metric/work-item read gating)
Rule-based blocked definition UI or migration (delivered in slice 01)

phases.name: Blocked-time history capture + per-item duration
phases.description: Implement the WorkItemBlockedTransition capture substrate, expose blockedSince on the team/portfolio WIP read surface, and render the 'blocked <N>d' badge with an honest sync-cadence tooltip.

steps.name: Backend infrastructure: WorkItemBlockedTransition entity, capture handlers, and migration
steps.description: TDD execution for the capture substrate. RED: add the owned WorkItemBlockedTransition entity, a DbSet with EF configuration/index, a WorkItemUnblocked domain event, and a migration test that fails because the table is absent; add handler tests that fail because no handler records or closes transitions. GREEN: implement WorkItemBlockedTransitionCaptureHandler (records EnteredAt on WorkItemBlocked) and WorkItemBlockedTransitionCloseHandler (sets LeftAt on WorkItemUnblocked); emit WorkItemUnblocked from WorkItemService.CollectDomainEvents when WasBlockedBeforeSync && !IsBlocked; register the new repository and handlers in Program.cs; generate SQLite and Postgres migrations using Create-Migration.ps1 AddWorkItemBlockedTransition.
steps.scenario_name: Migrations apply cleanly on SQLite/Postgres and capture handlers record/close WorkItemBlockedTransition rows
criteria: WorkItemBlockedTransition is an owned entity with Id, WorkItemId, EnteredAt, and nullable LeftAt.
EF configuration adds DbSet WorkItemBlockedTransitions, a cascade FK to WorkItem, and an index on (WorkItemId, EnteredAt).
WorkItemBlockedCaptureHandler creates exactly one open transition per WorkItemBlocked event (idempotent on replay).
WorkItemUnblockedCaptureHandler sets LeftAt on the single open transition for the work item.
WorkItemService emits WorkItemUnblocked when an item was blocked before sync and is no longer blocked after sync.
Program.cs registers IWorkItemBlockedTransitionRepository and both domain-event handlers.
Migrations are generated with Create-Migration.ps1 AddWorkItemBlockedTransition for both SQLite and Postgres providers.
dotnet build succeeds with zero warnings (TreatWarningsAsErrors).
dotnet test passes the new migration and handler tests.
deliverables: EF configuration, DbSet, and generated SQLite/Postgres migrations
Real-provider migration tests and handler unit/integration tests

steps.name: Read surface exposes blockedSince for a newly blocked item
steps.description: Enable the first backend acceptance scenario. Remove [Ignore] from A_newly_blocked_item_shows_how_long_it_has_been_blocked. Add BlockedSince to WorkItemDto; update TeamMetricsController.GetCurrentWipForTeam and FeaturesController.GetFeatureWorkItems to resolve the open transition's EnteredAt for blocked items and pass it to the DTO. Update Slice02BlockedDurationSpecifications.GivenABlockedItemObservedDaysAgo to seed an open WorkItemBlockedTransition with EnteredAt = SyncDay.AddDays(-daysAgo) so the fixture matches the production capture contract. RED: the scenario fails because blockedSince is missing. GREEN: the scenario passes and the timestamp reflects the seeded enter date.
criteria: The ignored scenario is enabled (remove [Ignore]).
WorkItemDto exposes a nullable BlockedSince property serialized as blockedSince.
TeamMetricsController and FeaturesController populate BlockedSince only for items where IsBlocked is true, using the open WorkItemBlockedTransition's EnteredAt.
GivenABlockedItemObservedDaysAgo seeds an open WorkItemBlockedTransition with the expected EnteredAt.
The scenario asserts blockedSince is present and carries the enter timestamp.
dotnet build and dotnet test are green.
deliverables: Updated acceptance-test fixture that seeds the capture entity

steps.name: Unblocked item clears blockedSince
steps.description: Enable An_unblocked_item_no_longer_shows_a_blocked_duration. The read surface must surface blockedSince as null when no open transition exists. If leave detection was not completed in 02-01, complete it here so a sync that unblocks an item closes the open spell. Optionally extend the scenario's Given helper to seed a closed transition. RED: the scenario fails because blockedSince is absent or non-null. GREEN: the scenario passes.
criteria: The ignored scenario is enabled.
An item with no open WorkItemBlockedTransition exposes blockedSince as null (property still present).
WorkItemService leave detection emits WorkItemUnblocked and the close handler sets LeftAt (if not already green from 02-01).
dotnet build and dotnet test are green.
deliverables: Unblock path closes the open blocked transition
Read surface returns null blockedSince for unblocked items

steps.name: First-observation blocked item shows no fabricated duration
steps.description: Enable A_first_observation_blocked_item_shows_no_duration_until_a_baseline_exists. A blocked item that existed before the feature shipped has no WorkItemBlockedTransition, so it must surface blockedSince as null rather than falling back to CurrentStateEnteredAt. RED: the scenario fails with a fabricated timestamp. GREEN: null.
criteria: The ignored scenario is enabled.
blockedSince is null when the item is blocked but no open transition exists (first observation).
No fallback to CurrentStateEnteredAt in the read surface.
dotnet build and dotnet test are green.
deliverables: First-observation contract enforced on the read surface

steps.name: blockedSince derives only from rule-matched items
steps.description: Enable An_item_that_does_not_match_the_blocked_rules_has_no_blocked_duration. Update GivenABlockedItemObservedDaysAgo to seed an open WorkItemBlockedTransition even for the non-blocked item. The controller must suppress blockedSince when IsBlocked is false, proving the single-definition invariant. RED: the scenario fails because a non-blocked item exposes a transition timestamp. GREEN: blockedSince is null.
criteria: The ignored scenario is enabled.
GivenABlockedItemObservedDaysAgo seeds an open transition for the non-blocked fixture item.
blockedSince is null for items where IsBlocked is false, even if an open transition row exists.
dotnet build and dotnet test are green.
deliverables: Single-definition invariant enforced on the read surface

steps.name: Frontend BlockedDurationBadge copy + tooltip
steps.description: TDD execution for the badge. Create BlockedDurationBadge component that receives blockedSince and blockedTerm. If blockedSince is null, render '-'. If present, render 'blocked <N>d' with a Tooltip title 'Approximate - based on sync cadence'. Add Vitest tests asserting the copy and tooltip. Wire the badge into WorkItemsDialog for blocked items. Update IWorkItem to include blockedSince?: Date | null so the typed contract matches the backend DTO. RED: new tests fail because the badge is absent. GREEN: tests pass and WorkItemsDialog surfaces the badge.
steps.scenario_name: Blocked duration badge renders 'blocked <N>d' and the sync-cadence tooltip; first-observation renders '-'
red_to_green_tests: BlockedDurationBadge :: renders '-' when blockedSince is null
BlockedDurationBadge :: renders 'blocked 2d' for a two-day-old blockedSince
BlockedDurationBadge :: shows the sync-cadence tooltip on hover
WorkItemsDialog :: renders the blocked duration badge for a blocked item
criteria: IWorkItem includes blockedSince?: Date | null.
BlockedDurationBadge renders '-' when blockedSince is null.
BlockedDurationBadge renders 'blocked <N>d' when blockedSince is set (N = whole days elapsed).
BlockedDurationBadge renders a Tooltip with title 'Approximate - based on sync cadence'.
WorkItemsDialog displays BlockedDurationBadge for blocked items.
pnpm test passes all Vitest suites including the new badge tests.
pnpm build completes with zero TypeScript, Biome, and Vite warnings.
Stryker TypeScript kill rate on BlockedDurationBadge.tsx is >= 80%.

walking_skeleton.scenario: A newly blocked item surfaces a per-item blocked duration on the team WIP read surface
walking_skeleton.coverage_estimate: End-to-end through the two driving adapters touched by the slice: the ASP.NET team metrics read port returns blockedSince, and the acceptance test drives it through WebApplicationFactory with real EF. The frontend badge rendering is covered in 02-06.

backend: dotnet test - all NUnit suites green, including the new BlockedDurationMigrationTests and WorkItemBlockedTransitionCaptureTests
Stryker.NET kill rate >= 80% on WorkItemService, WorkItemBlockedTransitionCaptureHandler, and WorkItemDto mutation scopes
frontend: pnpm test - all Vitest suites green, including BlockedDurationBadge.test.tsx
pnpm build - tsc -b, Biome, and vite build complete with zero errors and zero warnings
architecture: ArchUnitNET tests pass with no new violations (single IsBlocked path, WorkItemBlockedTransition distinctness)
sonarqube: No new issues (bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, security hotspots) introduced on the PR
